---
title: "stylometryEnsemble: Introduction"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
source("./00_source_project.R")
```

## 1. Confirm the raw KJV file exists

Place your raw file here:

```text
data/raw/kjv.csv
```

It should have this header:

```text
corpus_id,translation,book,chapter,verse,text
```

```{r check-file}
raw_path <- "./../data/raw/kjv.csv"
file.exists(raw_path)
```

## 2. Import raw verses

```{r import}
kjv <- readr::read_csv(raw_path, show_col_types = FALSE)
dplyr::glimpse(kjv)
```

## 3. Prepare section-level documents

```{r prepare-sections}
section_docs <- prepare_kjv_sections(kjv)

section_docs %>%
  dplyr::select(doc_id, section, macro_genre, n_verses, n_chapters, books)
```

```{r prepare-chapter}
chapter_docs <- prepare_kjv_chapters(kjv)

chapter_docs %>%
  dplyr::select(
    doc_id,
    book,
    chapter,
    section,
    macro_genre,
    n_verses
  )
```

## 4. Save prepared documents

```{r save-prepared}
dir.create("./../data/prepared", recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)
readr::write_csv(section_docs, "./../data/prepared/kjv_section_docs.csv")
readr::write_csv(chapter_docs, "./../data/prepared/kjv_chapter_docs.csv")
```

## 5. Run a lightweight ensemble

This first example excludes POS and readability so it runs quickly without optional packages.

```{r run-lightweight}
config <- default_config(
  include = c("char", "word", "stem", "function_words", "punctuation", "sentence", "lexical")
)

result <- run_stylometry_ensemble(section_docs, config = config)
round(result$overall_similarity, 3)


```

```{r nearest-neighbors}
result$nearest_neighbors
```


```{r run-lightweight chapters}
config <- default_config(
  include = c("char", "word", "stem", "function_words", "punctuation", "sentence", "lexical")
)

result <- run_stylometry_ensemble(chapter_docs, config = config)
round(result$overall_similarity, 3)
```


```{r nearest-neighbors}
result$nearest_neighbors
```
